title: Extending Naoko Akagis threefold persona concept to others
display_title: Extending Naoko Akagis threefold persona concept to others
wikitext:
[]: Extending Naoko Akagi's threefold persona concept to others             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Apr 16, 2009 4:45 am I've been thinking about this quite a bit. Obviously the mother archetype is a bit suspect for other characters who don't have kids, but you can see these aspects in Misato, too.. Quoting pretty loosely "I'm a horrible woman" (ep. 16), "I failed to play a mother for [Shinji/Asuka]".. and also, her diligence to her work towards the end of the series, overwhelming everything else in her character. The motherishness of just about all the major female characters in the show except for maybe Ritsuko is addressed at some point (even Asuka: "I never wanted to have kids, so why should I have to suffer this" (ep. 22)). Know what I mean? I'm also not sure what Anno meant with the song title "Three of me, one of someone else"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2009 6:51 am Did you perhaps forget about this? Episode 13 wrote: RITSUKO: The night before she died, my mother told me that the Magi are three aspects of herself. Herself as a scientist. Herself as a mother. Herself as a woman. The Magi is those three aspects struggling for dominance. She intentionally left in the dilemmas of human experience. Actually, each of the three programs are slightly different. I don't think I'll ever be a mother , so I don't understand her as a mother. But I respected her as a scientist. However, as a woman, I even hated her.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Apr 16, 2009 11:22 am "Three of me, one of someone else"

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Apr 16, 2009 12:09 pm in terms of the 3 Rei's could be applied as so (my interpretation): Rei I - the Woman/Girl aspect. She was too young to be a woman but she was just a little girl, not tainted by the worries of her job or the hardships of a mother. Rei II - "Job" aspect. She was only there for her job, with little ambition for anything else, save for pleasing Gendou, which she considered part of her duty. Rei III - Mother Aspect. She rejoined Lillith and thus completed herself as basically the mother of humanity.

[]:             Quote Post by Dartz » Thu Apr 16, 2009 1:01 pm It's like Passion, Caring, and Logic. The Woman, the Mother, the Scientist. Asuka, Shinji, Rei. Or those three psychologgy things. I don't know what ego, superego and id actually are... just their names.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Thu Apr 16, 2009 2:27 pm xtr00kvltcorex wrote: in terms of the 3 Rei's could be applied as so (my interpretation): Rei II - "Job" aspect. She was only there for her job, with little ambition for anything else, save for pleasing Gendou, which she considered part of her duty.

[]:             Quote Post by xtr00kvltcorex » Thu Apr 16, 2009 2:47 pm FallenTabris wrote: xtr00kvltcorex wrote: in terms of the 3 Rei's could be applied as so (my interpretation): Rei II - "Job" aspect. She was only there for her job, with little ambition for anything else, save for pleasing Gendou, which she considered part of her duty.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 16, 2009 4:47 pm I wonder if this talk about Rei may be a bit beside the point? Unless I'm forgetting something this piece of BGM had nothing to do with her. Anno could have used the title "Three of Me, One of Someone Else" for a Rei-related song if he wanted, but it's a song clearly connected to the Magi, so that's probably where the attention should be focused. Just a thought, anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by PuppetChaos » Thu Apr 16, 2009 6:57 pm Dartz wrote: I don't know what ego, superego and id actually are... just their names.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Thu Apr 16, 2009 11:19 pm FallenTabris wrote: Anno loves threes, though, that's for sure.
